metadata.title: Six Dots Make 31, Not 32: When Patterns Break

beats.narration_text: Halo. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of six dots make 31, not 32: when patterns break. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of six dots make 31, not 32: when patterns break
output: ✓ Six Dots Make 31, Not 32: When Patterns Break

beats.narration_text: Six Dots Make 31, Not 32: When Patterns Break is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. 1, 2, 4, 8, 16... then 31. The pattern breaks exactly when it seems most trustworthy.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Six Dots Make 31, Not 32: When Patterns Break

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Six Dots Make 31, Not 32: When Patterns Break. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: nSlider. Verify: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16... then 31. The pattern breaks exactly when it seems most trustworthy.. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Six Dots Make 31, Not 32: When Patterns Break. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: nSlider. Verify: 1, 2, 4,

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.

beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from yt-six-dots-31.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: six dots make 31, not 32: when patterns break. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 1 to 8. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 1 to 8

beats.narration_text: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16... then 31. The pattern breaks exactly when it seems most trustworthy.. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of six dots make 31, not 32: when patterns break. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Six Dots Make 31, Not 32: When Patterns Break simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
